An ethical hacker with a passion for cybersecurity, Aklile Mamo (MSIT '26) wants to give back by making systems safer for users in Africa and around the world. With fellowship support from The Upanzi Network at CMU-Africa, Mamo explains that she "wants to give back to the community that gave me this opportunity, which I really appreciate." Aklile is already giving back through her work on a research project with Jema David Ndibwile, assistant teaching professor at CMU-Africa, using a gamified technique of teaching cybersecurity concepts to children with autism. ["URL: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/cmu-africa-celebrates-student-excellence?trk=public_post
Content: The 2023 Lakshmi Subramanian CMU-Africa Student Excellence Awardees, 
Medhn Gebreegziabhar
  and 
Muhammad Omer
  were recognized from the Master of Science in Engineering Artificial Intelligence program. Hadush excelled in the Neurorobotics course and completed an impressive internship. Omer was honored for his exceptional performance as a Teaching Assistant.

Miquilina Anagbah

  and

  were recognized from the Master of Science in Engineering Artificial Intelligence program. Hadush excelled in the Neurorobotics course and completed an impressive internship. Omer was honored for his exceptional performance as a Teaching Assistant.

Miquilina Anagbah
, from the Master of Science in Electrical and Computer Engineering program, received the award for her outstanding leadership and organizational skills, as well as her research contributions.

Arisema Mihretu
 , a graduate from the Master of Science in Information Technology program, was recognized for her remarkable academic performance and contributions to student life. [...] Pamely ZANTOU

An ethical hacker with a passion for cybersecurity, Aklile Mamo (MSIT '26) wants to give back by making systems safer for users in Africa and around the world. With fellowship support from The Upanzi Network at CMU-Africa, Mamo explains that she "wants to give back to the community that gave me this opportunity, which I really appreciate." Aklile is already giving back through her work on a research project with Jema David Ndibwile, assistant teaching professor at CMU-Africa, using a gamified technique of teaching cybersecurity concepts to children with autism.
